ai_strategy_lead.name: OVPTL AI Advisory / Engineering AI for Society
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: University of California, Irvine has a public AI posture anchored in OVPTL AI Advisory / Engineering AI for Society. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: University of California, Irvine publishes AI policy or guidance with a permissive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at University of California, Irvine is evidenced through OVPTL AI Advisory / Engineering AI for Society and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: ZotGPT campus AI portfolio
initiatives.description: UCI's ZotGPT portfolio includes Chat, Creator, ClassChat, API access, training, a prompt library, and partner tools including Copilot, Gemini, and Zoom AI Companion.
initiatives.significance: ZotGPT is a mature campus-built platform pattern for secure, multi-model AI access at an R1 campus.

initiatives.title: DTEI Generative AI for Teaching and Learning
initiatives.description: UCI's Division of Teaching Excellence and Innovation hosts GenAI teaching events and an OVPTL AI Advisory Committee focused on instructional practices.
initiatives.significance: The work creates a governance and practice layer for AI-aware teaching rather than leaving classroom adoption wholly ad hoc.

computing_infrastructure.summary: UCI's Research Cyberinfrastructure Center lists HPC3 as a heterogeneous shared-computing cluster that, as of January 2026, includes 14 four-GPU V100 nodes, 18 four-GPU A30 nodes, four two-GPU A100 nodes, and three four-GPU L40S nodes, or 148 disclosed GPUs, alongside 11,600 CPU cores and InfiniBand networking.

narrative_summary: University of California, Irvine has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is OVPTL AI Advisory / Engineering AI for Society, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.
